preamble: Epenthesis of [k] typically occurs as excrescence between a velar nasal [ŋ] and a following dental or alveolar fricative.

examples.note: Excrescent [k] inserted between velar nasal [ŋ] and dental fricative [θ] in 'length'; reflects incomplete closure overlap during articulation.
Same process in 'strength'; common in many English dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Johnson, K. (2015). A Course in Phonetics (7th ed.). Cengage Learning.
